mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate crossing above 4.5 percent signals labor market deterioration and impending recession, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 65 percent of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand expansion, driving corporate revenue expectations higher and producing positive quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample quarterly return regressions over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production alongside accelerating inflation creates a stagflationary supply shock that forces central banks to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 70 percent of observations when both series rise simultaneously during high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPI inflation exceeding 3.5 percent triggers expectations of aggressive Federal Reserve tightening, which raises borrowing costs and equity discount rates, forcing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward return is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL surpasses the 3.5 threshold across 100+ observations.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising consumer spending capacity, which directly supports corporate earnings growth and yields positive monthly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between payroll growth and 20-day forward returns falls below 0.05 in every 3-year rolling window tested.

mechanism_narrative: Declining real GDP growth coinciding with rising federal funds rates indicates a late-cycle monetary tightening that suppresses aggregate demand, reducing corporate profit margins and generating negative quarterly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are positive in more than 60 percent of periods where GDP growth slows below 1 percent while rates rise.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the index of industrial production exceeding 0.5 percent signals immediate manufacturing contraction and weakening supply-side momentum, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative weekly S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 5-day returns in over 65 percent of instances where industrial production drops more than 0.5 percent month-over-month.
